title: Mobile Power Tool Storage Unit
summary: Free plans to build a customizable mobile storage unit that will keep your handheld power tools organized and easily accessible.
description: Free plans to build a customizable mobile storage unit that will keep your handheld power tools organized and easily accessible. This unit also has optional pegboards on each side for even more storage capacity.

steps.title: Cut shelves
steps.body: You can either use additional 1 x 12's to make the 17" long shelves or 3/4" plywood. I used up some leftover 3/4" plywood and shelving that I had in my woodpile to make mine.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes
steps.body: Drill three pocket holes on both short ends of two of the shelf pieces.

steps.title: Attach top and bottom
steps.body: Using glue and pocket screws, attach a shelf to the ends of the 1 x 12" to make the top and bottom of the shelving unit.

steps.title: Drill shelf pin holes
steps.body: Using the Kreg Shelf Pin Jig, drill shelf pin holes along the inside of the shelf sides.

steps.title: Attach back of shelving unit
steps.body: Cut a piece of the 1/4" plywood to fit the back of the shelving unit. Attach with glue and brad nails.

steps.title: Optional pegboard sides
steps.body: First, screw in small blocks of wood into the four corners of each side and also to the middle along the edges. (see photo)

steps.title: Pegboard sides part 2
steps.body: Cut and piece pegboard to fit each side of the shelving unit. Attach with staples or brad nails to blocks that were screwed in during the previous step.

steps.title: Wheels
steps.body: Add the wheels using 1-1/4" drawer front screws. I use this type of screw with the wide head because of the size of the holes on the wheels.

steps.title: Shelves
steps.body: Add the shelves and customize the placement of the shelves for your tools and storage needs.
